lesson: When a roadmap/changelog marks a release as shipped, verify and update VERSION, pyproject/package metadata, lockfile package version, README proof counts, and Current/Next roadmap lines in the same pass.
problem: Zephyr Workbench had v0.2.0 shipped in ROADMAP/CHANGELOG while VERSION and pyproject still said 0.1.0, and README listed an old test count.
solution: Run a release-docs consistency check before commit: compare VERSION, package metadata, lockfile, CHANGELOG, ROADMAP current status, README proof/test counts, then run the test suite and commit all synchronized files together.
task: Sync roadmap release status with package version and proof docs
validation: Updated zephyr-workbench to v0.2.0 across VERSION, pyproject.toml and uv.lock
Updated README test count to 126 after pytest reported 126 passed
Updated ROADMAP Current/Next lines to v0.2.0 shipped and v0.3.1 next
Committed and pushed zephyr-workbench commit 24ac599

lesson: When fixing an error, add it to mq-mcp learn only if it reveals a reusable pattern: documentation drift, release/version mismatch, contract mismatch, missing validation, safety boundary confusion, or workflow risk. Skip low-signal typos and one-off formatting unless they expose a repeated failure mode.
problem: Corrected errors can disappear into commit history, while indiscriminate logging would make learn memory noisy and less useful.
solution: After each verified fix, decide whether the error is reusable. If yes, store a learn record with the original mismatch, correction, files touched, validation commands, risk level, and tags. If no, leave it in the commit only.
task: Record high-signal corrected errors in learn memory
validation: Confirmed existing learn memory had one specific Zephyr roadmap/version-sync record
Added this broader rule through make_learning/record_learning so id, timestamp, allowed source/risk, and secret redaction follow the learn contract

lesson: When a command advertises --json, every wrapper in the call path must keep stdout machine-readable only; release/status checks that shell out to gh should request only supported JSON fields or degrade explicitly.
problem: mqlaunch hal ... --json appended the interactive pause prompt after delegated HAL commands, breaking JSON parsers. mq-hal release-brief also requested the unsupported gh release JSON field isLatest, causing a false Latest release warning and needs_review status.
solution: Skip mqlaunch pause prompts whenever any forwarded arg is --json, remove the unsupported isLatest field from gh release view, and add smoke tests for parse-clean HAL JSON plus supported gh field usage.
task: Keep JSON command wrappers parse-clean and external CLI fields stable

PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/mq-hal-pycache bash tests/release-brief-smoke.sh passed in mq-hal
PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/mq-hal-pycache ./release-check.sh passed in mq-hal
bin/mq-hal release-brief --json reported Latest release ok after removing isLatest

lesson: Editable installs can hide packaging bugs. If a CLI reads root files such as VERSION at import/runtime, release checks should verify wheel or sdist behavior, or the CLI should read the repo VERSION file in source checkouts and fall back to package metadata via importlib.metadata for wheel installs.
problem: mq-image-analyze passed local validation in editable repo mode, but the built wheel did not include the root VERSION file. The CLI imported mq_image_analyze.cli and tried to read parents[2]/VERSION, causing FileNotFoundError after normal wheel installation.
solution: Read root VERSION when it exists in a source checkout, and fall back to importlib.metadata when running from an installed wheel where root VERSION is absent. Add regression coverage for package metadata version lookup and keep release checks aware of wheel/install behavior.
task: Verify wheel installs for CLI release readiness
validation: Built mq-image-analyze wheel and confirmed it did not contain VERSION
Imported mq_image_analyze.cli from the unpacked wheel and reproduced FileNotFoundError before the fix
Added regression test that _read_version uses package metadata when root VERSION is absent

lesson: README edits often introduce small Markdown hygiene regressions: mixed unordered list markers (MD004) and long command/proof lines (MD013). After changing README content, run markdownlint and prefer dash list markers plus wrapped prose/commands.
problem: macos-scripts README had multiple asterisk unordered list items while markdownlint expected dash markers, and once those were fixed markdownlint surfaced long lines in proof and command examples.
solution: Normalize README unordered lists to dash markers, wrap long prose lines, split long shell examples with backslash continuations, then verify with npx markdownlint-cli2 README.md before committing docs changes.
task: Run markdownlint after README edits
validation: Converted reported README asterisk list markers to dash markers
Wrapped long proof, curl, SRM, and release-check lines
npx markdownlint-cli2 README.md reported 0 errors

lesson: When adding new mqlaunch entrypoints, the change touches four layers in a fixed order: (1) mq-agent-menu.sh — add to run_agent_command(), print_agent_menu(), and handle_agent_menu_choice(); (2) mqlaunch-command-mode.sh — add dispatch_cli_command() case + quick commands help text; (3) docs/COMMANDS.md — add one-liner in the review/delegation block; (4) tests/mq-agent-routing-smoke.sh — add grep assertions. Boundary invariant: shell scripts only call _run_agent; all logic stays in mq-agent.
problem: macos-scripts v0.6.0 needed mqlaunch entrypoints for perception check (mq-image-analyze) and stack health dashboard (mq-agent dashboard), but no pattern existed for wiring these through the four-layer mqlaunch command surface without putting any logic in shell scripts.
solution: Read the existing review/mcp-status entrypoints as the template. For each new command: add a named case to run_agent_command() that delegates to _run_agent; add a menu row + numbered handler; add a dispatch_cli_command() case; add to help text; add grep assertions to the routing smoke test; update docs. Keep every case identical in shape — the only variation is which mq-agent subcommand is forwarded.
task: Add mqlaunch entrypoints for perception check and stack health (v0.6.0)
validation: bash tests/mq-agent-routing-smoke.sh passed after adding perception and stack-health grep assertions
ROADMAP.md v0.6.0 all items marked [x]

lesson: The mq-mcp Release Gate v2 check_contracts_valid blocks on repos with neither docs/tool_contracts.json nor contracts/release_gate_v2.schema.json. Orchestration repos that expose no MCP tools must still carry contracts/release_gate_v2.schema.json as their ecosystem contract anchor — without it the gate shows BLOCKED/score=68. Copy the schema from mq-mcp/contracts/ and commit it.
problem: mq-agent release gate returned BLOCKED/68 because contracts/release_gate_v2.schema.json was missing. mq-agent has no docs/tool_contracts.json (it is not an MCP tool server), but check_contracts_valid requires at least one of the two files.
solution: Create contracts/ in mq-agent and copy release_gate_v2.schema.json from mq-mcp/contracts/. Gate goes from BLOCKED/68 to WARNING/86.
task: Unblock mq-agent release gate
validation: mq-agent release gate --json returned status: warning, score: 86
contracts_valid check changed from BLOCKED to PASS

lesson: mq-agent skill safety_class is inferred by _infer_safety() from SKILLS.md index text, not from individual SKILL.md files. To set a skill safety class, add 'read-only' or 'requires --approve' to the description line in SKILLS.md — changes only to SKILL.md detail files have no effect on skill list output.
problem: Five of six mq-agent skills showed safety_class: unknown. Adding 'Safety Class: read-only' to SKILL.md had no effect because _infer_safety() reads only from the SKILLS.md index.
solution: Append 'read-only' or 'requires --approve' to each skill description in SKILLS.md. After the fix all 6 skills report a known safety class.
task: Fix unknown safety_class for 5 mq-agent skills
validation: mq-agent skill list --json: all 6 skills have known safety classes
check-skill-contracts.sh passed
check-skill-quality.sh passed

task: Cherry-pick single commit from long-running feature branch onto protected main
problem: Cherry-picking fd3d8dc (feat: add learn extract-review command) from mq/update-documentation-20260604-232510 onto origin/main caused a conflict in main.py. The feature branch had accumulated skill commands and a shared helper _extract_mcp_text_result that did not exist on origin/main. The cherry-pick diff assumed those existed, causing NameError and incorrect conflict resolution that pulled in unrelated skill commands.
lesson: When cherry-picking a single commit from a long-running feature branch, shared helpers and sub-typers that the commit calls may only exist on the feature branch. Identify all new symbols the commit introduces calls to (e.g. _extract_mcp_text_result) and add them explicitly to the target branch in the same cherry-pick. Also check for sub-typers (skill_app) declared only on the feature branch and exclude them unless they are part of the scoped PR.
solution: Added _extract_mcp_text_result helper alongside the extract-review command in the cherry-pick. Removed skill_app sub-commands that were not on origin/main. Created clean branch feat/learn-extract-review-command from origin/main with only the three targeted changes: bridge method, CLI command, and tests.
validation: python3 -m py_compile mq_agent/main.py: no errors
uv run pytest tests/test_learn_extract_review_cmd.py: 5/5 passed
uv run pytest: 307/307 passed
PR #41 opened against main with clean diff

task: Fix used-before-def: registered_tools in validate_orchestration_contract
lesson: When a helper variable (registered_tools) is needed by multiple checks in a function, hoist its collection block to before the first check that uses it. Python static analysis cannot see that the late definition is always reached before use — it only sees textual order.
validation: mypy and Pylance no longer flag used-before-def on line 2459; 182 tests pass

task: Fix name-defined: _openai not imported in extract_coding_conventions
lesson: Lazy imports (import openai as _openai) inside a function scope are invisible to sibling code paths. Each function that calls _openai must import it locally — do not rely on a nearby function having done the import.
validation: Pylance and mypy no longer report _openai undefined at line 3024

task: Fix spec_from_file_location None-safety in _learn_engine loader
lesson: importlib.util.spec_from_file_location returns Optional[ModuleSpec]. Guard with "if spec is None or spec.loader is None: raise RuntimeError" before passing to module_from_spec and exec_module. Add # type: ignore[union-attr] on exec_module call after the guard to satisfy strict type checkers.
validation: Pylance reportArgumentType and reportOptionalMemberAccess cleared on lines 3686-3688

task: Fix attr-defined: ArchitectureMemory.get() returns str not object
lesson: ArchitectureMemory.get() returns Optional[str], so the result IS the raw content string. Accessing .content on it is wrong. Use full.splitlines() directly, not full.content.splitlines().
validation: mypy attr-defined and Pylance reportAttributeAccessIssue cleared at line 4305

lesson: Run: git diff $(git log -1 --format=%H docs/ORCHESTRATION_CONTRACT.md)..HEAD -- server.py | grep '@mcp.tool'. Each new @mcp.tool() function since the last contract update must be documented. Add a Tool groups table under the relevant cross-repo section (mq-agent or caller). Update Last updated date and version. Tools that are Class A/B with no persistent side effects do not need to be added to the side-effects table — only the tool groups table.

lesson: Added zephyr_validate, zephyr_review, zephyr_analyze, zephyr_diff as Class B subprocess proxy tools in server.py. Pattern mirrors hal_repo_report: binary via ZEPHYR_BIN env or ~/zephyr-workbench/.venv/bin/zephyr, path safety via resolve_allowed_local_file, rc 0 and 1 both accepted as success (zephyr returns rc=1 for valid-but-has-findings output). All 4 registered in tool_contracts.json and documented in ORCHESTRATION_CONTRACT.md v1.11.0.
task: Expose zephyr-workbench as callable mq-mcp tools
validation: Tested zephyr validate and review against citrix-igel-desktop-delivery.yaml — both returned correct output. Server restarted, tool_count=107 confirmed via /health. mq-agent run-tool zephyr_validate executed successfully.

lesson: Three patterns applied in one session: (1) CLI proxy tools need binary resolution via env var + fallback to ~/tool/.venv/bin/tool — never rely on bare name in PATH. (2) python-dotenv load_dotenv(override=False) at import time is the correct fix for uv-run env loss; .env changes take effect at next server start without any shell export. (3) When a CLI has --format json or --json flags, always expose those as separate mq-mcp tools returning dict — the text variants are good for human display but the JSON variants are what mq-agent can machine-read and chain.
task: Connect zephyr-workbench, fix repo-signal JSON coverage, and fix mq-mcp .env auto-load
validation: Verified: zephyr_validate/review/analyze/diff live via mq-agent. repo_signal_report, suggest, positioning all return schema-versioned JSON (report.v1, suggest.v1, positioning.v1). Server restarts cleanly with all env vars from .env — no manual export needed.

lesson: When adding or changing an mqlaunch terminal GUI/menu, treat it as a command surface change, not a standalone shell screen. Use terminal/menus/mq-hal-menu.sh as the reference implementation and ui/terminal-ui/mq-ui.sh as the shared UI library. New menus should support both sourced execution from mqlaunch.sh and standalone execution, render with surface_panel_header/surface_row/surface_split_row/surface_bottom, use read_main_choice when available, include b. Back and x. Exit launcher, and route actions to commands/bridges/tools rather than embedding business logic in menu files.
problem: mqlaunch has several terminal GUI surfaces. Without a recorded pattern, new menus can drift into one-off rendering helpers, inconsistent prompts, missing back/exit behavior, or logic embedded directly in presentation files.
solution: For GUI/menu work, inspect README, docs/COMMANDS.md, mqlaunch-command-mode.sh, mqlaunch.sh, relevant terminal/menus files, bridges/tools, and tests. Follow the mq-hal-menu.sh structure; keep menu files presentation+routing only; wire direct CLI mode and menu mode to the same behavior; update docs/help/tests when the command surface changes.
task: Build mqlaunch terminal GUI menus through the command-surface pattern
validation: Confirmed macos-scripts has a dedicated mqlaunch-command-surface skill for terminal GUI/menu work.
Confirmed terminal/menus/mq-hal-menu.sh documents the reference submenu pattern.
Confirmed existing menus source ui/terminal-ui/mq-ui.sh and render through surface_* helpers.

lesson: TUI-kod (splash, banner, fullscreen, read-prompter) som körs före subcommand-dispatchen bryter alla headless/icke-interaktiva anrop. Gate med [[ -t 0 && -t 1 ]] eller MQLAUNCH_HEADLESS överst i skriptet, före all TUI-init, så att argument-läge aldrig rör TTY. Mönstret gäller alla launcher-skript: mqlaunch (zsh), mongostart/Start-Meny.ps1 och term-setup (PowerShell).
